hub_subtitle: Business English for HR leadership in international meetings

lesson.menu_title: Now vs. Always — Present Simple vs. Present Continuous in HR Conversations
lesson.menu_desc: Contrasting routines and facts with actions happening now or temporarily -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Now vs. Always -- Simple vs. Continuous
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 12 -- Now vs. Always

listenings.text: Hi Murielle, it's Liam. Let me explain my situation this month. Ordinarily, I manage the payroll, and I delegate the reports to my team. I usually work from the Sao Paulo office, and I frequently review the numbers on Fridays. But this week is different. Right now, I am covering for Sofia because she is away, so I am temporarily handling two roles. At the moment, we are also reassigning a few analysts and rotating the on-call shifts. The support team is on standby all week. It is busy, but our normal workflow is still running well. I will send you the details later. Talk soon.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana. I want to explain how we ordinarily work and what is different now. Normally, the London team follows a fixed workflow: we process the appraisals, and each manager delegates the interviews. We occasionally rotate the tasks, but the routine is stable. However, this quarter we are changing a few things temporarily. At the moment, I am covering two teams, and we are reassigning some projects. The whole department is on standby for the audit. So, we usually work in a calm way, but right now everything is moving fast. I will call you again after the meeting. Thank you.
